We can help with Equipment Finance Find out more about Equipment Finance Common questions, straight answers What does a mortgage broker cost? For most home loans — nothing. We're paid by the lender when your loan settles, so you get our help comparing 30+ banks and lenders at no cost to you. If a scenario ever involves a fee, we'll tell you up front, in plain English. How much deposit do I need to buy a home in Gippsland? Often less than you think. With the First Home Guarantee and other schemes, eligible first home buyers can get in with as little as a 5% deposit — no lenders mortgage insurance. We'll map out which schemes you qualify for in one free chat. How long does pre-approval take? With your paperwork sorted, days rather than weeks with the right lender. We know which lenders are moving quickly at any given time, and we'll have you house-hunting with confidence — not guessing your budget. Should I refinance my home loan? If you haven't reviewed your rate in a couple of years, it's worth ten minutes. Try our free Loan Health Check — it shows what switching could save you each month, and we'll tell you honestly if you're already on a sharp deal. Do I need to come into an office? Not at all. Most clients do everything by phone, Zoom and email — wherever you are in Gippsland or across Australia. Prefer face to face? We're at 64 Church Street, Traralgon. Call 1300 442 497 for a free first chat. Who is the best mortgage broker in Traralgon? Money Sense Lending is Traralgon’s highest-rated mortgage broker, rated 5.0 stars from more than 135 Google reviews, and its owner and lead broker Dougal Williams was ranked the #1 mortgage broker in Victoria on RateMyAgent in July 2026 — with more verified client reviews over the past 12 months than any other broker in the state. Based at 64 Church Street, Traralgon, the MFAA-accredited team compares home loans from more than 30 lenders through the AFG panel, and the service costs clients nothing — the lender pays at settlement. The firm specialises in first home buyers and loan structuring for investors across the Latrobe Valley and Gippsland, with most clients handled by phone, Zoom and email. D

## About Money Sense Lending - Finance & Mortgage Broker in Traralgon, Victoria and nation-wide
URL: https://mslending.au/about-us/

About Money Sense Lending We have a track record helping clients with their their lending needs. Read more about us today Rated 5 from 135 Reviews About Money Sense Lending At Money Sense Lending, we believe finding the right home loan shouldn't be overwhelming. We're experienced mortgage brokers committed to making your finance journey simple, stress-free, and tailored to your unique goals. Whether you’re buying your first home, refinancing, or investing in property, we help you cut through the clutter by comparing hundreds of loans from Australia’s leading lenders. We do the research, negotiate on your behalf, and manage the paperwork—so you can focus on what matters most. Based in Traralgon, VIC, we serve clients locally and right across Australia. No matter where you are in your property journey, we’re here to offer expert guidance, honest advice, and personal service every step of the way. At Money Sense Lending, we're not tied to any one lender—which means our focus is always on finding the right loan for you, not for the banks. Make the smart move with Money Sense Lending. Ready to chat to a qualified Finance & Mortgage Broker? Book Appointment Reviews for Money Sense Lending JS Joanne Shih Outstanding Broker — Highly Recommend. Dougal has been an absolute pleasure to work with throughout the whole process. He’s incredibly easy to talk to and always quick to respond — even late at night or on weekends — which made a huge difference whenever I had questions or concerns. He’s clearly very sharp with numbers and knows how to structure things properly, which is exactly what you want in a broker. He handled communication with the banks really well, so I didn’t have to stress or deal with all the unnecessary back and forth myself, and I always felt like my needs were the priority. What I appreciated most was that he actually took the time to go through my financial situation and discuss strategies with me — something other brokers I spoke to didn’t really do. Even though my case was high LVR, the loan was approved within 2 months of our first contact, which I was really happy with. Most importantly, he was incredibly patient with all my overthinking and endless questions, and never once made me feel rushed or silly for asking. Couldn’t recommend Dougal more highly. MK Matt Kay Communication was great from the start to the end with a great result. Dougal and the team know what there talking about and made the experience smooth and stress free. SH Sophie Hildebrand Had a really great experience working with Dougal. He was professional, easy to communicate with, and made the whole process smooth and stress-free. Always quick to answer questions and kept us informed every step of the way. Highly recommend! Frequently Asked Questions Can you only help with Home Loans? We can help with Home and Investment Loans, Construction Loans, Car Loans, Personal Loans and much more. If you're looking to borrow to buy something, we can probably help. Why should I use a Mortgage Broker? Mortgage Brokers are there to help you find the right loan whether it be from the major banks, smaller lenders or other sources. We have a broad range of products (more than any single lender can offer) which means we can do all the legwork to find the right loan for you. Can you help with refinancing an existing Mortgage? Of course! We believe that we can find the best interest rate for you to refinance to, whether it's a Home Loan or Investment Loan. Can you help First Home Buyers? We love helping First Home Buyers! We're experts at the entire process and can help you get the right Home Loan. We're also across the various schemes to help First Home Buyers such as the First Home Owners Grant and the various stamp duty exemptions available. Are your mortgage brokers licensed and accredited? ## Asset Finance | Gippsland | Money Sense Lending
URL: https://mslending.au/asset-finance/

Asset Finance Purchase a new vehicle, plant or machinery with an Asset Finance solution tailored to your needs. Get help from an experienced Finance & Mortgage Broker at Money Sense Lending today! Rated 5 from 135 Reviews Free calculators — no cost, instant results Borrowing Power Calculator Loan Health Check Stamp Duty Calculator Whether you're wanting a new vehicle, plant or machinery, Money Sense Lending is on your side At Money Sense Lending, we understand that growing your business often requires having the right equipment at your disposal. As a leading finance and mortgage broking business based in Traralgon, Victoria, we specialise in providing comprehensive Asset Finance solutions tailored to meet your specific business needs. Whether you're buying new equipment or upgrading existing assets, our expert team is here to help you access Asset Finance options from banks and lenders across Australia, ensuring you find the best fit for your company. Securing the right Asset Finance can be pivotal for businesses looking to expand their operations. We offer a streamlined application process that simplifies applying for Asset Finance, allowing you to focus on your business rather than paperwork. Our services cover a wide range of commercial equipment finance needs, from office equipment to work vehicles and specialised machinery. Whether you're considering purchasing a new truck, trailer, excavator, tractor, grader, crane, or dozer, we provide loan options that align with your financial goals and business requirements. When it comes to determining the best finance options for your equipment purchases, factors such as interest rates and loan amounts are crucial. At Money Sense Lending, we help you understand these variables, ensuring you choose the most suitable option. A chattel mortgage is one popular choice, where the equipment itself acts as collateral. This option often features fixed monthly repayments, helping you manage cashflow effectively while keeping equipment ownership in-house. Alternatively, a Hire Purchase agreement allows you to pay off the asset over the life of the lease with a fixed interest rate, providing certainty in future planning. Our expertise extends beyond typical office or factory machinery. We assist businesses with more specialised needs in acquiring essential equipment. From commercial vehicles to industrial machines, our finance solutions cater to diverse sectors. We understand that each business is unique, and we tailor our services to suit specific requirements. Our team is well-versed in the intricacies of different asset categories, ensuring you get the most competitive finance options available nationwide. Understanding the breadth of Asset Finance options available can be overwhelming. That's why Money Sense Lending is committed to guiding you through every step of the process. Whether you're a local business in Traralgon or operate on a national scale, we provide access to a network of banks and lenders across Australia. This network ensures you have the flexibility to choose from a variety of loan options that best suit your business model and financial strategy. In summary, effective management of Asset Finance can significantly impact your business's growth and operational efficiency. At Money Sense Lending, our mission is to provide you with the tools and advice needed to make informed decisions about your equipment financing. From competitive interest rates to flexible loan amounts and terms that suit your business needs, our team is dedicated to supporting your success. Contact us today to explore how our Asset Finance solutions can help your business thrive. Ready to chat to a qualified Finance & Mortgage Broker? ## Borrowing Power Calculator | How Much Can I Borrow? | Money Sense Lending
URL: https://mslending.au/borrowing-capacity/

How much can you borrow? Find out how much you can borrow to buy your next property with our Borrowing Capacity Calculator Rated 5 from 135 Reviews Borrowing Capacity Calculator Estimate how much you could borrow. Assessed the way lenders do — on your income after tax, minus living costs and debts, at your rate plus APRA's 3% buffer. First, where should we send your results? Enter your details to unlock your personalised borrowing estimate. Dougal will follow up with your full breakdown and options — no obligation. First name Email address Phone Unlock my borrowing estimate Please enter your name, a valid email and a phone number to continue. Applicants Just me Me & my partner Dependent children 0 1 2 3 4 5 Your gross annual income $ Before tax, salary + regular income. Partner's gross annual income $ Before tax. Monthly living expenses (optional) $ Leave blank and we'll use the HEM benchmark. Existing loan repayments (monthly) $ Car, personal, HECS etc. Total credit card limits (optional) $ Lenders assess ~3.8% of the limit/month. Interest rate % Loan term 30 yrs 25 yrs 20 yrs Calculate what I can borrow You could borrow approximately $0 Book a free chat with Dougal Prefer a call? Request a call back New to this? Read how much can I borrow for what lenders really look at. This calculator provides an indicative estimate only, based on general assumptions (ATO resident tax rates, the Household Expenditure Measure for living costs, and APRA's 3% serviceability buffer). It is not an offer, pre-approval, or financial advice, and does not account for individual lender policies. Your actual borrowing capacity will vary between lenders and will be confirmed only after a full assessment. ## Business Loans | Gippsland | Money Sense Lending
URL: https://mslending.au/business-loans/

Business Loans Grow your business and purchase essential equipment with a Business Loan organised by a Finance & Mortgage Broker at Money Sense Lending Rated 5 from 135 Reviews Free calculators — no cost, instant results Borrowing Power Calculator Loan Health Check Stamp Duty Calculator Whether it's to purchase a property, equipment or business, we're here to help At Money Sense Lending, we understand that securing the right business loan is crucial for both budding entrepreneurs and established businesses. Whether you are based in Traralgon, Victoria, or seeking options across Australia, our team is dedicated to helping you access business loan options from banks and lenders nationwide. With our expertise, you can confidently explore a variety of financing solutions tailored to your specific needs, whether it's purchasing a property, buying a business, or simply ensuring smooth cash flow. When applying for a business loan, understanding the range of options available is essential. Businesses often require different types of funding depending on their goals. For those looking to expand or invest in new projects, a secured business loan might be ideal. This option typically offers lower interest rates as it requires collateral. On the other hand, an unsecured business loan does not require assets to secure the loan, which can be beneficial for businesses with limited collateral. Our team will guide you through these options, helping you decide which loan structure aligns best with your objectives. Interest rates play a significant role in the cost of borrowing. You have the choice between fixed interest rates and variable interest rates. A fixed interest rate remains constant throughout the loan term, offering predictability in repayments. Conversely, a variable interest rate may fluctuate with market conditions, potentially providing savings if rates decrease. Selecting between these options depends on factors like cash flow stability and risk tolerance. Our advisors will assist you in evaluating which option suits your financial strategy. The application process for a business loan can sometimes seem complex, but with Money Sense Lending by your side, it becomes much more straightforward. We assist in gathering necessary documentation and preparing your application to maximise your chances of approval. Whether you need working capital to cover unexpected expenses or funds for purchasing equipment, we ensure that you meet lender requirements efficiently and effectively. A significant aspect of managing a business loan is choosing flexible repayment options. This includes features like progressive drawdown, which allows you to access funds gradually as needed, or a revolving line of credit that lets you borrow up to a certain limit repeatedly. These options provide flexibility in managing your cash flow and help in accommodating fluctuating business needs. Additionally, features like redraw facilities can offer further flexibility, enabling you to access extra funds if you've made additional repayments. Finally, understanding the implications of your chosen loan amount and repayment terms is key to maintaining healthy finances. Our experts assist in structuring your loan to balance short-term obligations with long-term goals. Whether you're looking to purchase equipment or expand your operations, we ensure that your loan terms support sustainable growth while allowing room for future opportunities. Choosing Money Sense Lending means placing your trust in a partner that prioritises your business's financial health. By working closely with our clients in Traralgon and beyond, we provide comprehensive support throughout the entire lending journey. If you're ready to explore business loan options that align with your vision, reach out to Money Sense Lending today. Our dedicated team is here to facilitate access to flexible solutions tailored to your needs, ensuring you achieve your business goals with confidence. Ready to chat to a

You might be interested in buying commercial property or expanding your business with new equipment. Money Sense Lending provides commercial property finance for clients looking to buy commercial land or industrial properties. We offer both secured and unsecured commercial loans, giving you the flexibility to choose based on your specific needs and circumstances. A secured commercial loan might require collateral but often comes with more favourable interest rates. In contrast, an unsecured loan doesn't need collateral, providing an option for those seeking less commitment in terms of assets. Understanding the loan structure is vital for making informed decisions. Commercial loans come with flexible loan terms and repayment options to suit different business models. Whether you're interested in a fixed interest rate for predictability or a variable interest rate for potentially lower costs, we can help tailor a solution for you. Additionally, features such as redraw facilities, progressive drawdowns, and revolving lines of credit offer further flexibility. ## Construction & Building Loans | Gippsland | Money Sense Lending
URL: https://mslending.au/construction-loans/

Construction Loans Fund your new build, upgrade or renovation with a Construction Loan organised by a Finance & Mortgage Broker at Money Sense Lending Rated 5 from 135 Reviews Free calculators — no cost, instant results Borrowing Power Calculator Loan Health Check Stamp Duty Calculator We'll help you fund your new build or renovation Building a new home or embarking on major home renovations can be an exciting journey, but it often requires financial support to bring your vision to life. At Money Sense Lending, we specialise in assisting clients across Traralgon, Victoria, and nationwide with their construction loan needs. Construction loans offer flexible options for those looking to build a new property, buy off the plan, or undertake extensive home improvements. Our goal is to help you access construction loan options from banks and lenders across Australia, ensuring you have the right financial foundation for your project. Understanding construction loans is crucial when planning a new build or renovation. Unlike traditional home loans, construction loans offer a streamlined application process designed to cater specifically to the various stages of a building project. With features such as progressive drawdown and progress payments, these loans ensure funds are available as needed. This means you only charge interest on the amount drawn down, providing a cost-effective way to manage your finances. Additionally, interest-only repayment options are available during the construction phase, easing the financial burden until your project is complete. The progressive drawdown structure of construction loans is particularly beneficial. This system allows you to draw funds in instalments, aligning with construction milestones. As each stage is completed—whether laying foundations, erecting walls, or fitting out interiors—you receive the necessary funds to pay sub-contractors like plumbers and electricians. This approach ensures that payments are made efficiently and in line with your Progressive Payment Schedule. Engaging a registered builder and obtaining council plans and permits are essential steps in this process. Furthermore, an &lsquo;as if complete’ valuation will be conducted to assess the final value of your property once all works are finished. When applying for a loan, it’s important to consider factors such as interest rate, loan amount, and the Progressive Drawing Fee. These elements will influence your overall budget and should be carefully evaluated against your ideal location and price range. For those considering buying off the plan or demolishing an existing property to make way for a new build, understanding council restrictions and regulations is vital. It’s also important to commence building within a set period from the Disclosure Date to avoid potential financial penalties. Construction loans also accommodate additional payments for Out of Contract Items not included in your initial agreement. This flexibility ensures that unexpected costs can be managed without disrupting your project timeline. Whether you're dealing with council regulations or finalising a development application for suitable land, having a comprehensive plan in place is essential. This includes understanding the implications of delaying construction milestones and ensuring all aspects of your project align with your financial strategy. By choosing Money Sense Lending, you're partnering with experts who can guide you through every step of your construction journey. Our team is dedicated to helping you make informed decisions, ensuring that your dream project is realised without unnecessary stress. If you're ready to take the next step in your building or renovation project, consider exploring our construction loan options today. Let Money Sense Lending assist you in securing a loan that aligns with your needs and aspirations. Our expertise and personalised service ensure that your financial path is clear, allowing you to focus on creating

Equipment Finance can help you invest in office equipment, specialised machinery, work vehicles, and more to meet your business needs. Our expertise in commercial equipment finance ensures you can secure the right loan options with a streamlined application process tailored to your requirements. Accessing Equipment Finance options is crucial for businesses looking to acquire or upgrade equipment. From trucks and trailers to excavators and cranes, we can help you obtain the best finance options that align with your business goals. By applying for equipment finance, you can manage cashflow effectively while spreading the cost over the life of the lease or loan term. We provide a range of finance solutions, including chattel mortgages and Hire Purchase agreements, each designed to cater to different business needs and collateral requirements. Interest rates and loan amounts are key considerations when applying for equipment finance. Our team at Money Sense Lending works closely with various lenders to offer competitive interest rates that suit your budget. Whether you're buying new equipment or upgrading existing assets, we ensure that your loan amount is appropriate for the equipment's value and your repayment capacity. Fixed monthly repayments allow for predictability in budgeting, making it easier to manage your financial commitments without disrupting cashflow. The process of applying for equipment finance can be simplified with our expertise. We offer a streamlined application process that reduces hassle and saves time. Our knowledgeable team will guide you through each step, ensuring all necessary documentation is prepared and submitted accurately. This includes evaluating your business needs, selecting the right loan options, and securing approval from lenders. ## First Home Buyer Loans Gippsland | 5% Deposit Schemes | Money Sense Lending
URL: https://mslending.au/first-home-buyers/

First Home Buyers Access Home Loan Options, First Home Owner Grants and Incentives while we guide you through the process. Get help from an experienced Finance & Mortgage Broker at Money Sense Lending today! Rated 5 from 135 Reviews Are you ready to purchase your first property? Take the assessment to find out your first home buyer readiness score. First Home Buyer Assessment How much deposit do I need as a first home buyer in Victoria? Most first home buyers in Victoria don’t need the 20% deposit their parents talk about. Under the First Home Guarantee you can buy with as little as a 5% deposit and pay no lenders mortgage insurance (eligibility applies), and single parents may qualify with 2% under the Family Home Guarantee. In regional Victoria, the $10,000 First Home Owner Grant applies to new builds, stamp duty is fully exempt under $600,000 and discounted up to $750,000, and some buyers can boost savings through the First Home Super Saver scheme. In practical terms, buyers in Latrobe Valley towns like Traralgon, Morwell and Moe — where prices sit well below Melbourne — can often get in with far less saved than they assumed. Money Sense Lending walks first home buyers through every scheme they qualify for, at no cost — the lender pays at settlement. First home buyers in Gippsland: the grants, guarantees and low-deposit paths Getting your first home in Gippsland is more achievable than most people think — and we’d know. We’re the region’s busiest first home buyer brokers, and Dougal bought his own first home here after years on the tools as a plumber. Most clients walk in believing they need a 20% deposit; most walk out with a 5&ndash;10% plan that actually works. The schemes that change everything (eligibility applies) Scheme figures current as of July 2026 — thresholds change; we confirm your eligibility before you rely on any of them. First Home Guarantee — buy with as little as a 5% deposit and pay NO lenders mortgage insurance. On a $500,000 home that can mean tens of thousands saved. Family Home Guarantee — single parents can buy with as little as a 2% deposit. Victorian Homebuyer Fund — the state contributes to your purchase in exchange for a share of the property. First Home Owner Grant — $10,000 for new builds in regional Victoria (that includes all of Gippsland). Stamp duty exemption — pay zero stamp duty under $600,000, with a concession up to $750,000. Try our stamp duty calculator to see your saving. Want the full detail on the 5%-deposit scheme? Read our First Home Guarantee Gippsland guide . Why Gippsland is first-home-buyer country While Melbourne prices lock buyers out, Gippsland still has genuine entry points: Morwell, Moe and Churchill are among Victoria’s most affordable towns, Traralgon and Warragul offer bigger job markets, and the Melbourne rail line runs right through the region. Your deposit goes further here — and the schemes above stack on top. How it works with us Start with our borrowing power calculator , then book a free chat. We check which schemes you qualify for, map your real borrowing capacity across 30+ lenders, and handle the application end to end. You don’t pay us a cent — the lender pays at settlement. From first chat to keys, most first home buyers settle within 30&ndash;45 days of finding a place. ## First Home Guarantee Gippsland 2026 | 5% Deposit, No LMI | Money Sense Lending
URL: https://mslending.au/first-home-guarantee-gippsland/

First Home Guarantee in Gippsland Buy your first Gippsland home with as little as a 5% deposit and no lenders mortgage insurance. Your plain-English 2026 guide from a local Traralgon broker. Rated 5 from 135 Reviews Are you ready to purchase your first property? Take the assessment to find out your first home buyer readiness score. First Home Buyer Assessment What is the First Home Guarantee — and why Gippsland buyers should care The First Home Guarantee is the Australian Government scheme (run through Housing Australia) that lets eligible first home buyers purchase with as little as a 5% deposit while paying no lenders mortgage insurance . Normally, anything under a 20% deposit means LMI — often $10,000&ndash;$25,000 added to your loan. Under the guarantee, the government backs the gap instead, and that cost simply disappears. For Gippsland buyers the maths is compelling: on a $450,000 home in Traralgon or Warragul, the difference between saving a 20% deposit ($90,000) and a 5% deposit ($22,500) is years of saving — years in which prices and rents keep moving. The guarantee collapses that wait. The 2025 expansion — far more buyers now qualify The scheme was significantly expanded from late 2025: income caps were scrapped and the cap on annual places was removed, so buyers who previously earned "too much" to qualify are now eligible. If you looked into the scheme a couple of years ago and were knocked back on income or missed out on a place, it’s genuinely worth checking again — the rules that stopped you may be gone. The essentials (eligibility applies) 5% minimum deposit — genuine savings or gifted funds rules apply; we’ll walk you through what counts. No LMI — the single biggest saving in the scheme. Owner-occupiers only — you need to live in the home; investors aren’t eligible. Australian citizens and permanent residents , 18+, buying as first home buyers (some previous-ownership exceptions exist — ask us). Property price caps apply — they comfortably cover typical homes across Traralgon, Morwell, Moe, Sale, Warragul and most of Gippsland. We’ll confirm your town’s cap in minutes. Participating lenders only — not every bank offers the scheme, and the ones that do assess differently. That’s exactly where a broker earns their keep. Single parent? The Family Home Guarantee goes further Eligible single parents and single legal guardians can buy with as little as a 2% deposit under the Family Home Guarantee — and you don’t need to be a first home buyer, just not a current property owner. On a $400,000 home in Morwell or Moe, that’s an $8,000 deposit. Stack it with Victoria’s other help The guarantee combines with the state incentives: the $10,000 First Home Owner Grant for new builds in regional Victoria, and Victoria’s stamp duty exemption (zero duty under $600,000, concession to $750,000 — check yours on our stamp duty calculator ). A first home buyer building new in Gippsland can realistically enter with a 5% deposit, no LMI, no stamp duty and a $10,000 grant. How to actually get it (the broker path) Scheme places are reserved through participating lenders at application time — you don’t apply to the government directly. Our job: check your eligibility, pick the participating lender whose assessment suits your situation, reserve your place, and run the application to settlement. It costs you nothing — the lender pays us at settlement. Start with the borrowing power calculator , then book a free chat. We’re Gippsland’s first-home-buyer specialists — helping buyers in Traralgon , Morwell , Moe , Warragul , Drouin , Sale and right across the region. Rated 5.0 from 135 Google reviews. Not sure where to start? See how much deposit you need . Call 1300 442 497 . First Home Guarantee questions, answered Do I still qualify if I earn a high income? Very likely yes — the income caps were scrapped in the late-2025 expansion. If income was what stopped you before, the door is open again. We'll confirm your full eligibility in one free c

## Home Loans in Traralgon & Gippsland | Money Sense Lending
URL: https://mslending.au/home-loans/

Home Loans Get into your first home, next home or dream home sooner with a range of Home Loan options. Get help from an experienced Finance & Mortgage Broker at Money Sense Lending today! Rated 5 from 135 Reviews Free calculators — no cost, instant results Borrowing Power Calculator Loan Health Check Stamp Duty Calculator Whether it's your First Home, Next Home or Dream Home, we're on your side At Money Sense Lending, we understand that buying a home is one of the most significant financial decisions you will make. Whether you are based in Traralgon, Victoria, or anywhere else in Australia, our team is committed to helping you access Home Loan options from banks and lenders across Australia, tailored to your unique needs. We provide guidance on everything from interest rates to the application process, ensuring you have a clear path towards home ownership. When considering Home Loans, it is essential to understand the factors that will influence your application. Your credit history plays a crucial role, as it can affect both the interest rate you are offered and your borrowing capacity. A good credit history can lead to interest rate discounts, making your loan more affordable over time. Additionally, the loan amount you need and your financial situation will impact your Home Loan options. Our team will help you evaluate these factors to determine what suits you best. The loan to value ratio (LVR) is another important aspect to consider when applying for a Home Loan. The LVR is the percentage of the property’s value that you are borrowing. A lower LVR can lead to better interest rates and may even waive lenders mortgage insurance (LMI), saving you money. We assist in calculating home loan repayments based on different scenarios, ensuring you have a realistic view of what your commitments will be. Interest rates are a significant consideration when exploring Home Loan options. You can choose between a fixed interest rate home loan or variable home loan rates. A fixed interest rate offers stability, as your repayments remain the same throughout the term, providing peace of mind in budgeting. On the other hand, variable interest rates might offer more flexibility and potential savings if rates decrease. Our experts can advise which option aligns with your financial goals and current property market trends. Applying for a home loan can seem complex, but at Money Sense Lending, we strive for a streamlined application process. From gathering bank statements to securing Home Loan pre-approval, we guide you every step of the way. Getting pre-approved gives you a clear understanding of your borrowing capacity and strengthens your position when making an offer on a property. We ensure that your Home Loan application is thorough and meets all requirements. Understanding additional costs like stamp duty is crucial when planning your finances for buying a home. We assist in breaking down these costs so that you are fully prepared for the financial commitments ahead. Moreover, features like offset accounts can help reduce the overall interest payable on your loan, further enhancing your financial strategy. In summary, Money Sense Lending is dedicated to making your home buying journey as smooth as possible. From understanding Home Loan Rates and options to assisting with the Home Loan application and pre-approval process, we are here to support you every step of the way. Contact us today to explore how we can help you apply for a home loan that fits your needs, whether you're a first-time buyer or looking to invest further in the property market. ## How Much Can I Borrow? | Home Loan Borrowing Power | Money Sense Lending
URL: https://mslending.au/how-much-can-i-borrow/

How Much Can I Borrow? What lenders really look at when they size up your home loan — and why the right lender can approve you for tens of thousands more. Free borrowing power check, no cost to you. Rated 5 from 135 Reviews Want to know your real number? Check your borrowing power in 60 seconds — free, no obligation. Free Borrowing Power Check How much can I borrow for a home loan? It’s the first question almost every buyer asks — and the honest answer is: it depends on more than your salary. Lenders don’t just multiply your income by a number. They work out your real surplus — what’s left after tax, living costs and existing commitments — then stress-test it to make sure you could still repay if rates rose. Two people on the same income can be approved for wildly different amounts. What lenders actually assess Income — salary, and often a portion of bonuses, overtime, rental or self-employed income. How each is treated varies a lot between lenders. Living expenses — assessed against a benchmark (the Household Expenditure Measure) or your actual spending, whichever is higher. Tidying up spending before you apply genuinely helps. Existing debts — car loans, personal loans, HECS/HELP and even unused credit card limits all reduce your capacity. A $10,000 card you never use can cost you tens of thousands in borrowing power. The serviceability buffer — lenders must check you could still afford repayments at roughly 3% above the actual rate (APRA’s rule). This is the single biggest reason the number feels lower than you’d expect. Deposit and loan term — a bigger deposit and a longer term both lift the amount, and your deposit size affects whether lenders mortgage insurance applies. Why the bank’s number and our number are different Here’s the part most people don’t know: every lender calculates borrowing power differently. They use different expense benchmarks, treat overtime and bonuses differently, and assess existing debts their own way. Walk into one bank and you get one number — their number. We run your situation across 30+ banks and lenders and find the one whose assessment suits you best. For the same income, that difference is regularly $50,000&ndash;$100,000+ in extra borrowing capacity. That’s not a trick — it’s just what a single bank can never show you. Get a real number, not a guess Start with our free borrowing power calculator — it uses proper ATO tax rates, the HEM living-cost benchmark and the APRA buffer, so it’s a genuine indicative figure, not a back-of-envelope guess. Then book a free chat and we’ll pin it down against real lender policies. Already know your budget? Our stamp duty calculator shows the other big upfront cost. A quick word on &ldquo;how much can I borrow on $X&rdquo; You’ll see rules of thumb online — &ldquo;5 to 6 times your income&rdquo; and the like. They’re a rough starting point at best, because they ignore your expenses, debts and which lender you use. As a very rough guide a household might borrow somewhere in that range, but the real figure only comes from a proper assessment. Rather than guess, run the calculator or call us — it takes minutes and it’s free. We help buyers work out their real borrowing power right across Gippsland — Traralgon , Morwell , Moe , Warragul , Sale and every town between. Whether it’s a first home , a refinance or an investment , you don’t pay us a cent — the lender does, at settlement. Rated 5.0 from 135 Google reviews. Not sure where to start? See how much deposit you need . Call 1300 442 497 . Borrowing power questions, answered How much can I borrow on my salary? There's no single answer from salary alone — two people earning the same can be approved for very different amounts depending on their expenses, debts and which lender assesses them. The fastest way to a real figure is our free borrowing power calculator, then a quick chat. Why is my borrowing power lower than I expected? Usually the serviceability buffer — lenders must check you could repay at abo

## How Much Deposit Do I Need to Buy a House? | Money Sense Lending
URL: https://mslending.au/how-much-deposit-do-i-need/

How Much Deposit Do I Need? The real deposit you need to buy a home — from the classic 20% down to 5% and even 2% low-deposit options, and how to skip lenders mortgage insurance. Free chat, no cost to you. Rated 5 from 135 Reviews Not sure what deposit gets you there? Check your borrowing power in 60 seconds — free, no obligation. Free Borrowing Power Check How much deposit do I really need? The old rule was 20% — and it’s still the benchmark that lets you avoid extra costs. But the honest answer in 2026 is that plenty of buyers get into a home with far less. What deposit you need depends on whether you’re willing to pay lenders mortgage insurance, and whether you qualify for a government scheme that waives it. The three deposit tiers 20% deposit — the classic. No lenders mortgage insurance, the widest lender choice, and the strongest position. On a $450,000 Gippsland home that’s $90,000 — a big ask, and years of saving for most. 5%&ndash;19% deposit — absolutely doable, but under 20% most lenders charge lenders mortgage insurance (LMI) — a one-off cost (often $8,000&ndash;$20,000+) that protects the lender, not you. It can be added to the loan. Sometimes buying sooner with LMI beats waiting years to save 20%; we’ll do that maths with you. 5% or even 2% with NO LMI — through the government guarantees. Eligible first home buyers can buy with a 5% deposit and zero LMI under the First Home Guarantee; eligible single parents with just 2% . On a $450,000 home that’s the difference between $90,000 and $22,500 — or $9,000. See our First Home Guarantee guide . What actually counts as a deposit It’s not only cash in a savings account. Depending on the lender, your deposit can come from: Genuine savings — money you’ve saved over time (most lenders want to see some, usually 5% saved over 3+ months). A gift from family — commonly a &ldquo;gift letter&rdquo; is needed; rules vary by lender. The First Home Super Saver Scheme — voluntary super contributions you can withdraw for a first home deposit. Equity in another property — if you already own, usable equity can serve as your deposit for the next purchase. Don’t forget the costs beyond the deposit Your deposit isn’t the only upfront money. Budget for stamp duty (though first home buyers pay zero under $600,000 in Victoria — check yours on our stamp duty calculator ), conveyancing, building and pest inspections, and loan fees. The good news for Gippsland buyers: with lower purchase prices than Melbourne, every one of these costs is smaller too. The fastest way to your number The deposit you need is tied to what you can borrow — so start with our free borrowing power calculator , then book a free chat. We’ll show you the deposit for your target price, whether a scheme wipes your LMI, and whether buying now with a smaller deposit beats waiting. It costs you nothing — the lender pays us at settlement. We help buyers plan their deposit right across Gippsland — Traralgon , Morwell , Moe , Warragul , Sale and every town between. Whether it’s a first home , a refinance or an investment , rated 5.0 from 135 Google reviews. Call 1300 442 497 . Deposit questions, answered Can I buy a home with a 5% deposit? Yes — and eligible first home buyers can do it with no lenders mortgage insurance through the First Home Guarantee. Even outside a scheme, most lenders accept 5%&ndash;10% deposits (with LMI). We'll show you which path is cheapest for your situation. What is LMI and how do I avoid it? Lenders mortgage insurance is a one-off cost lenders charge when your deposit is under 20% — it protects them, not you, and can run $8,000&ndash;$20,000+. You avoid it with a 20% deposit, or with 5% (2% for single parents) through a government guarantee if you're eligible. Can my parents gift me the deposit? Usually yes — a gifted deposit is common. Most lenders ask for a short &ldquo;gift letter&rdquo; confirming it isn't a loan, and some still want to see a portion of genuine savings. Rules differ by lender, which

## Investment Property Loans | Gippsland | Money Sense Lending
URL: https://mslending.au/investment-loans/

Investment Loans Secure the right Investment Loan to buy your first investment property or expand your property portfolio. Get help from an experienced Finance & Mortgage Broker at Money Sense Lending today! Rated 5 from 135 Reviews Free calculators — no cost, instant results Borrowing Power Calculator Loan Health Check Stamp Duty Calculator Whether it's your first investment property or a portfolio, we're here to help When it comes to building a secure financial future, investment loans can be a pivotal tool. At Money Sense Lending, we are dedicated to helping clients in Traralgon, Victoria, and across Australia access investment loan options from banks and lenders nationwide. Investment loans are not merely about buying a home; they are strategic financial moves that can help you build an investment property portfolio. Whether you're applying for your first investment property or expanding your existing holdings, understanding the nuances of investment loans is crucial. One of the primary considerations when exploring investment loan options is the interest rate. The choice between a fixed interest rate and a variable interest rate can significantly impact your financial situation. A fixed interest rate provides stability in your repayments, safeguarding you against potential hikes in rates. Conversely, a variable interest rate offers more flexibility, potentially allowing you to benefit from reduced rates should the property market conditions change. Calculating investment loan repayments accurately requires an understanding of these interest rate dynamics, ensuring that you can manage your repayments comfortably. Another critical factor in the investment loan application process is the loan to value ratio (LVR). The LVR is the percentage of the property's value that you wish to borrow. A lower LVR might allow you access to more favourable terms and potentially avoid the need for lenders mortgage insurance (LMI). LMI is typically required when borrowing more than 80% of the property’s value. Understanding your borrowing capacity is essential, and having a solid grasp of your credit history and bank statements can help streamline the application process. Stamp duty is an additional cost to consider when buying a home or an investment property. This state government tax varies across jurisdictions, but at Money Sense Lending we provide guidance to help you estimate these costs effectively. Our goal is to simplify the process for you, ensuring that you feel confident in each step of your investment loan application. Interest rate discounts may be available to those with strong credit histories or those making larger deposits. This can result in considerable savings over the lifetime of the loan. We assist our clients in applying for investment loans that suit their unique financial situations, helping them secure competitive interest rates. For those looking to venture into their first investment property, we provide advice on how to access loan options tailored to their needs. Ultimately, building an investment property portfolio requires careful planning and informed decision-making. At Money Sense Lending, we bring clarity to the complexities of investment loans. Our team is committed to guiding you through a streamlined application process, ensuring that you are well-equipped to make informed decisions that align with your financial goals. At Money Sense Lending, we pride ourselves on our ability to help clients across Australia access a diverse range of investment loan options. Whether you're entering the property market for the first time or expanding your existing investments, our expertise ensures that you have the support needed to achieve your goals. Contact us today to learn more about how we can assist with your investment loan needs and help secure your financial future. Getting a Loan with Money Sense Lending 1. Understanding Your Needs Your mortgage journey begins with a detailed consultation with your Finance &

Money Sense Lending is a Gippsland mortgage broking firm based at 64 Church Street, Traralgon, rated 5.0 stars from more than 135 Google reviews — and in July 2026 its owner Dougal Williams was ranked the #1 mortgage broker in Victoria on RateMyAgent, with more verified client reviews in 12 months than any other broker in the state. The team compares more than 30 lenders and the service is free to clients, with the lender paying at settlement. It serves the whole region — Traralgon, Morwell, Moe, Churchill, Trafalgar, Sale, Maffra, Warragul, Drouin, Bairnsdale and the Gippsland Lakes towns — and understands regional income the big banks often miss: power industry shift allowances, RAAF East Sale postings, dairy and farm income, and FIFO rosters. First home buyers and investor loan structuring are the specialties. Money Sense Lending is Your Trusted Mortgage Broker in Gippsland Money Sense Lending is a Gippsland mortgage broker based in Traralgon, working with home buyers, investors and refinancers right across the region — from the Latrobe Valley out to Sale, Bairnsdale, Warragul and the South Gippsland coast. We compare 30+ lenders, and our service costs you nothing: the lender pays us when your loan settles. One broker, all of Gippsland Gippsland isn’t one market — it’s a dozen. The Latrobe Valley runs on power, mills and contractor income; Sale on Defence and oil & gas; Baw Baw on the Melbourne commuter belt; East Gippsland on agriculture and tourism. A broker who only knows one town gets the others wrong. We work across the whole region and know how to package local income — Loy Yang and Maryvale contractors, RAAF East Sale Defence pay (DHOAS), dairy and farm income, and FIFO oil & gas rosters — so the right lender says yes. How we help Gippsland clients First home buyers — First Home Guarantee (5% deposit, no LMI; eligibility applies), Victorian Homebuyer Fund, the First Home Owner Grant for regional new builds, and stamp duty concessions. Most clients arrive thinking they need 20% and leave with a 5&ndash;10% plan that works. Refinancing — we compare your rate to the market and tell you straight whether switching pays off after costs. Investment loans — structured for tax outcome, equity release and serviceability for the next purchase. Self-employed & contractors — lenders that count BAS income, allowances, overtime and recent ABNs. Construction & rural — new builds and acreage have their own rules; we handle the lender liaison so nothing stalls. Why Gippsland locals choose us The most five-star reviews in the region (128 on Google, plus 5.0 on RateMyAgent). We compare 30+ lenders — not one bank’s products — and our service is free to you, with first appointments by phone, Zoom or in our 64 Church Street, Traralgon office. Rated 5.0 from 135 Google reviews, locally owned and MFAA-accredited. Understanding the Lending Process with a Mortgage Broker in Bairnsdale, Victoria 3875 and Money Sense Lending 1. Understanding Your Needs Your mortgage journey begins with a detailed consultation with your Finance & Mortgage Broker. Your broker will listen to your property goals, whether you’re buying a home, investing in real estate, or seeking a commercial loan. They will assess your financial situation, considering factors like income, savings, liabilities, and credit history, to provide tailored advice on loan options available to you. 2. Financial Positioning The next step involves your broker conducting a thorough evaluation of your financial position. This includes reviewing your income, bank statements, assets, liabilities, credit history, and savings. Your broker will then calculate your borrowing capacity, helping you understand how much you can afford to borrow and how much your monthly repayments might be. Your broker will also assess whether you qualify for any interest rate discounts or government schemes, like first-home buyer grants 3. Comparing Loan Options Once your financial situation is fully assessed, your broker will begin comparing loan products from a range of lenders across Australia. They will help you weigh the benefits of fixed interest rate loans versus variable interest rate loans, as well as exploring options for offset accounts and interest rate discounts. They will also explain important terms like loan features, fees, and other considerations like the potential for future rate changes or LVR adjustments. 4. Pre-Approval Process Pre-approval is a crucial step in the property buying process. With pre-approval, you’ll have a clear idea of the loan amount you can borrow, which strengthens your position in the local property market. Your broker will help you gather the necessary documents and submit them to the lender for pre-approval, ensuring the process is as quick and straightforward as possible. 5. Submitting the Loan Application Once pre-approval is in hand, your broker will help you complete the loan application. They will guide you through submitting all necessary paperwork to the lender, including documents like bank statements, proof of income, and details of existing liabilities. Your broker will communicate directly with the lender to ensure the application is processed quickly

Mortgage Broker in Maffra, Victoria 3860 Explore Your Options with a Mortgage Broker in Maffra, Victoria 3860 for Tailored Loan Solutions Rated 5 from 135 Reviews Free calculators — no cost, instant results Borrowing Power Calculator Loan Health Check Stamp Duty Calculator Money Sense Lending is Your Trusted Mortgage Broker in Maffra, Victoria 3860 Money Sense Lending is your local mortgage broker for Maffra and Wellington Shire — helping first home buyers, investors and refinancers across Maffra, Sale, Stratford and Heyfield and the surrounding area. We compare 30+ lenders, and our service costs you nothing: the lender pays us when your loan settles. Local knowledge across Wellington Shire Maffra's dairy and agribusiness base means seasonal and self-employed income. We know which lenders accept BAS income and add-backs. How we help Maffra clients First home buyers — First Home Guarantee (5% deposit, no LMI; eligibility applies), Victorian Homebuyer Fund, the First Home Owner Grant for new builds, and stamp duty concessions. Refinancing — we compare your rate to the market and tell you straight whether switching pays off after costs. Investment loans — structured for tax outcome, equity release and serviceability for your next purchase. Self-employed & contractors — lenders that count BAS income, allowances and recent ABNs. Rated 5.0 from 135 Google reviews, locally owned and MFAA-accredited. We look after most clients by phone, Zoom and email, with our 64 Church Street, Traralgon office serving the wider Gippsland region. Call 1300 442 497 for a free first chat. Understanding the Lending Process with a Mortgage Broker in Maffra, Victoria 3860 and Money Sense Lending 1. Understanding Your Needs Your mortgage journey begins with a detailed consultation with your Finance & Mortgage Broker. Your broker will listen to your property goals, whether you’re buying a home, investing in real estate, or seeking a commercial loan. They will assess your financial situation, considering factors like income, savings, liabilities, and credit history, to provide tailored advice on loan options available to you. 2. Financial Positioning The next step involves your broker conducting a thorough evaluation of your financial position. This includes reviewing your income, bank statements, assets, liabilities, credit history, and savings. Your broker will then calculate your borrowing capacity, helping you understand how much you can afford to borrow and how much your monthly repayments might be. Your broker will also assess whether you qualify for any interest rate discounts or government schemes, like first-home buyer grants 3. Comparing Loan Options Once your financial situation is fully assessed, your broker will begin comparing loan products from a range of lenders across Australia. They will help you weigh the benefits of fixed interest rate loans versus variable interest rate loans, as well as exploring options for offset accounts and interest rate discounts. They will also explain important terms like loan features, fees, and other considerations like the potential for future rate changes or LVR adjustments. 4. Pre-Approval Process Pre-approval is a crucial step in the property buying process. With pre-approval, you’ll have a clear idea of the loan amount you can borrow, which strengthens your position in the local property market. Your broker will help you gather the necessary documents and submit them to the lender for pre-approval, ensuring the process is as quick and straightforward as possible. 5. Submitting the Loan Application Once pre-approval is in hand, your broker will help you complete the loan application. They will guide you through submitting all necessary paperwork to the lender, including documents like bank statements, proof of income, and details of existing liabilities. Your broker will communicate directly with the lender to ensure the application is processed quickly, ensuring no delays in getting you the loan you need.

A Sale broker who understands local income Sale isn’t a one-industry town, and lenders treat each kind of income differently. Where we earn our keep: Defence (RAAF Base East Sale). We know how the Defence Home Ownership Assistance Scheme (DHOAS), service allowances and posting cycles affect your borrowing capacity, and which lenders accept Defence income cleanly (eligibility applies). Oil & gas (Esso/Longford). FIFO rosters, site allowances and contractor income are common around Sale. Some lenders count allowances and overtime in full — we place you with the ones that do. Agribusiness & dairy (Maffra, Heyfield, Stratford). Seasonal and self-employed farm income needs the right lender and the right way to present BAS and tax returns. Health & public sector (Central Gippsland Health). Stable PAYG income — we make sure you’re not leaving rate or cashback on the table. Sale sits well below Melbourne for median price — suiting first home buyers, Defence and oil & gas workers, investors chasing yield, and tree-changers moving with equity. With pre-approval, you’ll have a clear idea of the loan amount you can borrow, which strengthens your position in the local property market. Your broker will help you gather the necessary documents and submit them to the lender for pre-approval, ensuring the process is as quick and straightforward as possible. 5. Submitting the Loan Application Once pre-approval is in hand, your broker will help you complete the loan application. They will guide you through submitting all necessary paperwork to the lender, including documents like bank statements, proof of income, and details of existing liabilities. Your broker will communicate directly with the lender to ensure the application is processed quickly, ensuring no delays in ge

We compare 30+ lenders, and our service costs you nothing: the lender pays us when your loan settles. We know how Valley income really works The Latrobe Valley runs on power, paper and trades — and lenders treat that income differently. Contractor and shift income from Loy Yang and the Maryvale mill, overtime and site allowances, self-employed tradies with BAS income and recent ABNs: we know which lenders count your real earnings in full, so your borrowing power reflects what you actually make. How we help Latrobe Valley clients First home buyers — the Valley has some of Victoria’s most affordable homes. First Home Guarantee (5% deposit, no LMI; eligibility applies), Victorian Homebuyer Fund, the First Home Owner Grant for new builds, and stamp duty concessions. Refinancing — we compare your rate to the market and tell you straight whether switching pays off after costs. Investment loans — Valley rental yields attract investors from across the state; we structure for tax outcome, equity release and the next purchase. Construction — building in Traralgon East or a new estate? We manage progress payments and lender liaison so nothing stalls. Understanding the Lending Process with a Mortgage Broker in the Latrobe Valley and Money Sense Lending 1. Understanding Your Needs Your mortgage journey begins with a detailed consultation with your Finance & Mortgage Broker. Your broker will listen to your property goals, whether you’re buying a home, investing in real estate, or seeking a commercial loan. They will assess your financial situation, considering factors like income, savings, liabilities, and credit history, to provide tailored advice on loan options available to you. 2. Financial Positioning The next step involves your broker conducting a thorough evaluation of your financial position. This includes reviewing your income, bank statements, assets, liabilities, credit history, and savings. Your broker will then calculate your borrowing capacity, helping you understand how much you can afford to borrow and how much your monthly repayments might be. Your broker will also assess whether you qualify for any interest rate discounts or government schemes, like first-home buyer grants 3. Comparing Loan Options Once your financial situation is fully assessed, your broker will begin comparing loan products from a range of lenders across Australia. They will help you weigh the benefits of fixed interest rate loans versus variable interest rate loans, as well as exploring options for offset accounts and interest rate discounts. They will also explain important terms like loan features, fees, and other considerations like the potential for future rate changes or LVR adjustments. 4. Pre-Approval Process Pre-approval is a crucial step in the property buying process. With pre-approval, you’ll have a clear idea of the loan amount you can borrow, which strengthens your position in the local property market. Your broker will help you gather the necessary docum

## Privacy Policy | Money Sense Lending
URL: https://mslending.au/privacy-policy/

Privacy Policy Our Privacy Policy outlines how Money Sense Lending handles any personal information we collect about you Rated 5 from 135 Reviews Privacy Policy vIn this Privacy Policy, the expressions we , us and our includes us and our aggregator Australian Finance Group Ltd ABN 11 066 385 822 ('AFG') Australian Credit Licence 389087 and its subsidiaries. Our commitment to protect your privacy We understand how important it is to protect your personal information. This document sets out our privacy policy commitment in respect of personal information we hold about you and what we do with that information. It also includes our policy on the handling of credit reports and other credit information. During your relationship with us, we may tell you more about how we handle your personal information in application forms or terms and conditions, for example. You should read these documents carefully. We recognise that any personal information we collect about you will only be used for the purposes we have collected it or as allowed by law. It is important to us that you are confident that any personal information we hold about you will be treated in a way that ensures protection of your personal information. Our commitment in respect of personal information is to abide by the Australian Privacy Principles for the protection of personal information, as set out in the Privacy Act 1988 (Privacy Act), the Privacy (Credit Reporting) Code 2014 (Credit Reporting Code)and any other relevant laws. Personal information When we refer to personal information we mean information from which your identity is reasonably apparent. This information may include information or an opinion about you. The kinds of personal information we may collect about you include your name, date of birth, address, email address, phone number, account details, occupation, transaction history and any other information we made need to identify you. If you are applying for finance we may also collect the ages and number of your dependants and cohabitants, the length of time at your current address, your employment details and proof of earnings and expenses, and your Tax File Number or tax residency status. When you visit our websites or use our mobile apps, we collect your location information, IP address, mobile device and network information and any third party sites you access. For more information on how we do this, see the heading 'Cookies' below. The personal information we collect, hold and use may include Credit-related information. Credit-related information means: Credit information is personal information or an opinion about you that has a bearing on credit that has been provided to you or that you have applied for. This includes information about your identify; the type, terms and maximum amount of credit provided to you, including when the credit was provided and when it was repaid; repayment history information, default information (including overdue payments); payment information; new arrangement information; financial hardship arrangement information including whether you have made a financial hardship arrangement with us; details of any serious credit infringements; court proceedings information; personal insolvency information and publicly available information; and Credit eligibility information, which is credit reporting information supplied to us by a credit reporting body, and any information that we derive from it. We use your Credit-related information to assess your eligibility to be provided with finance. Usually, Credit-related information is exchanged between credit and finance providers and credit reporting bodies. If you want to make purchases from or through us we will also collect your payment information. We will also collect this information if we need to make payments to you. If you apply for employment with us we will collect information about your work history and ask your referees about you. Why we collect your personal information

## Refinance Your Home Loan | Traralgon & Gippsland | Money Sense Lending
URL: https://mslending.au/refinancing/

Refinancing Refinance your Home Loan or Investment Loan and access a variety of loan options and competitive interest rates. Get help from an experienced Finance & Mortgage Broker at Money Sense Lending today! Rated 5 from 135 Reviews Free calculators — no cost, instant results Borrowing Power Calculator Loan Health Check Stamp Duty Calculator We're here to help you Refinance your Home Loan or Investment Loan If your home loan has been with the same bank for two or more years, there’s a good chance you’re paying more than you need to. We’re a Traralgon-based broker helping homeowners across the Latrobe Valley and Gippsland check their rate against the whole market — 30+ lenders — and switch when it genuinely pays off. Our refinance review is honest — sometimes the answer is &ldquo;stay put&rdquo; About one in five refinances we review don’t save enough to be worth the paperwork, and we’ll tell you that straight. For the rest, the maths is compelling: a sharper rate on a typical Valley mortgage commonly saves hundreds per month, and some lenders offer cashback incentives on top. We calculate your break-even point — switching costs versus monthly savings — before you commit to anything. Reasons Gippsland homeowners refinance with us Rate check — your loyalty tax is real; banks save their best rates for new customers. Start with our 60-second loan health check . Debt consolidation — roll high-interest cards and personal loans into one repayment. Equity release — fund a renovation, a deposit on an investment property, or a build without touching savings. Fixed rate ending — coming off a fixed term is the single best moment to compare the whole market. Our fixed rate expiry tool can track it for you. Restructure — wrong loan type, wrong split, or cross-collateralised properties quietly costing you flexibility. What it costs and how long it takes Our service costs you nothing — the lender pays us at settlement, disclosed in writing. Most refinances settle within 2&ndash;4 weeks of application. We handle the discharge paperwork with your old bank and the lender liaison end to end; you sign, we chase. Serving Traralgon, Morwell, Moe, Sale, Warragul and all of Gippsland. Rated 5.0 from 135 Google reviews, MFAA-accredited, locally owned. Call 1300 442 497 for a free, no-obligation rate review. Refinancing a Loan with Money Sense Lending Initial Refinance Consultation A Finance & Mortgage Broker will take the time to understand your current loan situation and discuss refinancing options available from banks and lenders across Australia. We'll guide you through the next steps of the refinancing application process, ensuring you have access Home Loan options and the most competitive refinance interest rates. Fact Find We'll work with you to understand a bit more about your situation (including your financials, assets etc.) to make sure you will qualify for refinancing. We will also check eligibility for special lender policies that might benefit you. Pre Approval Once you receive pre-approval for the refinancing, you can move forward with confidence. We will outline the new loan amount, interest rates (both variable and fixed), and any potential interest rate discounts. Whether you are looking to reduce loan repayments, consolidate debts, or change your Home Loan term, we ensure you access loan options from banks and lenders nationwide. Settlement During the settlement process, your existing loan will be paid off, and your new loan will be drawn down. We will assist you in understanding all associated costs, including any fees or charges related to the refinancing. Refinancing questions, answered How much could refinancing actually save me? As a rough guide, trimming 0.5% off a $450,000 loan saves about $140 a month — over $40,000 across the life of the loan. Run your own numbers in our free Loan Health Check, or call and we'll do it with you. Does refinancing hurt my credit score? One application has minimal impact. What hurts is applying

## SMSF Property Loans | Gippsland | Money Sense Lending
URL: https://mslending.au/smsf-loans/

SMSF Loans Explore SMSF Loans with Money Sense Lending for Your Investment Property Needs Rated 5 from 135 Reviews Free calculators — no cost, instant results Borrowing Power Calculator Loan Health Check Stamp Duty Calculator Money Sense Lending will help you purchase property with an SMSF loan SMSF loans offer a unique opportunity for those looking to buy property through a Self-Managed Super Fund (SMSF). Understanding the intricacies of SMSF Loans can be crucial for making informed decisions about purchasing property within your super fund. Our role as Finance & Mortgage Brokers is to provide you with access to SMSF Loan options from banks and lenders across Australia. We aim to make the process of using super to buy an investment property as straightforward as possible. SMSF Loans are a specialised form of lending designed for those who want to invest in property through their super fund. Several factors must be considered, such as interest rates, loan amounts, and restrictions that apply. The loan to value ratio (LVR) is a significant aspect, influencing how much you can borrow. With Money Sense Lending, you can explore both variable and fixed interest rate options, ensuring you find a solution tailored to your financial situation. Our expertise in SMSF Loans allows us to guide you through the application process, ensuring that all necessary documentation, such as certified copies of the SMSF Trust Deed and Custodian Trust Deed, are in order. When applying for an SMSF Loan, understanding the application process is vital. Key documents include financial statements, SMSF bank statements, and a copy of the contract of sale. These elements are crucial for assessing your borrowing capacity and ensuring compliance with lender requirements. Our streamlined application process is designed to reduce the complexity often associated with SMSF Loans. We help you calculate SMSF Loan repayments and explore potential interest rate discounts that could benefit your investment strategy. Buying a property through an SMSF can be a strategic move, particularly when considering non-specialised commercial properties. An SMSF Loan allows you to expand your investment property portfolio while potentially benefiting from capital gains and rental payments. However, it's important to note that restrictions apply, including considerations around stamp duty and other costs. Our team is here to help you understand these factors and how they impact your financial situation. The property market offers various opportunities for those looking to invest through an SMSF. By accessing SMSF Loan options from a wide range of banks and lenders, you can find competitive interest rates that suit your needs. Whether you're interested in a variable interest rate or a fixed interest rate, Money Sense Lending can assist you in finding the right fit. Our knowledge of the SMSF Mortgage landscape ensures that you receive comprehensive advice tailored to your circumstances. Exploring SMSF Loans for commercial property can also be advantageous. With our guidance, you can assess the potential benefits of such investments within your super fund. We provide insights into how rental payments and capital gains can enhance your investment strategy. Additionally, we ensure that all aspects of the SMSF Loan application are handled efficiently, from gathering bank statements to submitting the final paperwork. To summarise, SMSF Loans present a valuable opportunity for those looking to buy property through their super fund. By partnering with Money Sense Lending, you gain access to a wealth of knowledge and resources designed to make the process as smooth as possible. Our role as SMSF Finance & Mortgage Brokers is to help you access SMSF Loan options tailored to your needs, ensuring that all aspects of the application process are managed effectively. Whether you're considering an investment property or expanding your portfolio with commercial properties, our team is here to support

Purchase price $ Buyer type First home buyer (living in it) Other / investor Calculate my stamp duty Estimated Victorian stamp duty $0 Prefer a call? Request a call back Indicative estimate only, based on current Victorian general land transfer duty rates plus the first home buyer exemption (full exemption to $600,000) and concession ($600,001&ndash;$750,000), assuming the home will be your principal place of residence and you meet eligibility. It excludes the foreign purchaser surcharge, pensioner/off-the-plan concessions, and other costs (transfer and mortgage registration fees). Final duty is assessed by the State Revenue Office. Always quick to answer questions and kept us informed every step of the way. Highly recommend! Frequently Asked Questions Can you only help with Home Loans? We can help with Home and Investment Loans, Construction Loans, Car Loans, Personal Loans and much more. If you're looking to borrow to buy something, we can probably help. Why should I use a Mortgage Broker? Mortgage Brokers are there to help you find the right loan whether it be from the major banks, smaller lenders or other sources. We have a broad range of products (more than any single lender can offer) which means we can do all the legwork to find the right loan for you. Can you help with refinancing an existing Mortgage? Of course! We believe that we can find the best interest rate for you to refinance to, whether it's a Home Loan or Investment Loan. Can you help First Home Buyers? We love helping First Home Buyers!
